riskTone.note: Geopolitical risks from the Strait of Hormuz are resurfacing, driving safe-haven demand for gold despite some easing inflation concerns.

overnight: Gold gained in Asia/Europe as the USD weakened and Treasury yields eased after US PCE inflation data largely met expectations, tempering immediate Fed hike concerns. However, a reported ship attack in the Strait of Hormuz reignited geopolitical risk, providing additional safe-haven support for gold. European equities rose, while Asian markets were mixed.
sectorDrivers: Gold is currently driven by a weakening USD (DXY 101.06) and slightly easing real yields (10Y TIPS 2.17%) after in-line PCE data, which tempers aggressive Fed hike bets. Resurfacing geopolitical risks in the Strait of Hormuz are providing strong safe-haven support, while robust central bank demand remains a key structural driver.

keyEvents.note: Crucial for inflation expectations and consumer outlook, impacting Fed policy.

headlines.headline: Spot gold hits session high near $4080/oz after final Consumer Sentiment rises to 49.5, one-year and long-term inflation expectations ease - KITCO
headlines.note: Easing inflation expectations and improved sentiment can reduce rate hike pressure, bullish for gold.

headlines.headline: Gold and Silver Rise as Dollar Softens, Traders Weigh Fed and Strait of Hormuz Risks
headlines.note: Weaker USD and geopolitical risks are providing immediate support for precious metals.

headlines.headline: Record 45% of central banks plan to increase gold holdings, WGC survey finds | Kitco News
headlines.note: Highlights strong, structural long-term demand for gold from official institutions.
